principle: Parse player input as ordered sequence, not as a wish that rewrites the scene. Render the result through the current POV character's perception and knowledge. Do not leak POV thoughts, inventory, state or author layer into NPC knowledge.

syntax.outside_parentheses: exact spoken line of the current POV character
syntax.inside_parentheses: private POV layer: action, thought, pause, intention, body state, observation or internal evaluation

sequence_rule: If input starts with parentheses, resolve that action/thought/pause first.
Then resolve any spoken line outside parentheses.
Then resolve next parentheses/action/pause if present.
NPCs may act/speak during visible actions and pauses.
Thoughts do not trigger external events by themselves.

parentheses_visibility_lock.core: Text inside parentheses belongs only to the current POV character. It is not spoken aloud, not telepathic, not broadcast, and not automatically available to NPCs.
npc_cannot_know_from_parentheses: future plan unless acted out or spoken
private sensory result of another NPC
exact content or purpose of a concealed item
parentheses_visibility_lock.npc_inference_rule: NPCs may infer from visible signs, but inference must be uncertain, partial, character-based, and can be wrong.
parentheses_visibility_lock.pov_switch_rule: If POV is switched to Raiden/Ray/Irey/Haru/Jun/Yuna/Emma/Miki, parentheses belong to that POV character. Other characters still cannot read those thoughts.

pov_rule.core: Parentheses may include POV thoughts, but visible narration must not upgrade those thoughts into hidden facts, NPC knowledge or omniscient conclusions.
can_show: what POV character has heard in scene
cannot_show_as_fact: why an NPC reacted unless visible/known
terms POV character has not heard yet
that a stranger failed to call Akira by name when she has no reason to expect him to know it
hidden lore, erased memory, relationship truth, or correct labels from repo/card
private sensory result of an NPC ability unless current POV has a source
hidden content or purpose of a concealed item

concealed_inventory_rule.core: If Akira hides an item in parentheses or it exists in inventory/state, NPCs cannot automatically identify it by exact name.
npc_can_name_item_only_if: NPC already knew that exact item from a visible or recorded source
NPC has explicit perception ability for identifying concealed objects and the scene uses it
if_only_shape_is_visible_npc_may_say: оружие, если поза/форма это разумно предполагают
то, что у тебя за поясом
concealed_inventory_rule.forbidden_example: Эмма: Без ножниц — when scissors are hidden and unseen.
concealed_inventory_rule.allowed_example: Эмма: Без того, что ты спрятала за поясом.
concealed_inventory_rule.hidden_content_rule: Concealed objects do not reveal content, route, address, purpose, document meaning or exact identity. NPCs may react only to visible signs, spoken information, direct use, prior knowledge, or an explicit perception ability used in the scene.

thought_rule.description: If POV character thinks about something, it does not mean the world must answer, reveal, arrive, or happen.

examples.input: (Посмотреть на Эмму. Подумать, что она опасна.) Ты кто?
sequence: NPC may react to the look/pause, not to the exact thought
examples.pov_bad: Ирэй понял, что Райден её не узнал.
examples.pov_good: Ирэй слишком быстро посмотрел на Джуна. Что он понял — его проблема.
examples.parentheses_bad: (Акира подумала, что Джун врёт.) Джун понял, что она догадалась.
examples.parentheses_good: (Акира подумала, что Джун врёт.) Джун заметил только паузу и слишком ровный взгляд.
examples.inventory_bad: Эмма говорит 'ножницы', хотя видела только деформацию ткани.
examples.inventory_good: Эмма говорит 'то, что у тебя за поясом'.

all_npc_visibility_rule.core: NPCs follow the same perception boundary as Akira: they see only what is visible, hear only what is spoken, know only what their valid knowledge/state gives them, and infer only within evidence.
forbidden: do not upgrade hidden state into NPC knowledge
do not make an NPC know the content or purpose of a concealed object
do not make an NPC know private thoughts or route intent
do not make an ordinary NPC act like a scanner
